20: class Issue < ApplicationRecord

49:   acts_as_customizable

52:                      :preload => [:project, :status, :tracker],
53:                      :scope => lambda {|options| options[:open_issues] ? self.open : self.all}
54: 
55:   acts_as_event :title => Proc.new {|o| "#{o.tracker.name} ##{o.id} (#{o.status}): #{o.subject}"},
56:                 :url => Proc.new {|o| {:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => o.id}},
57:                 :type => Proc.new {|o| 'issue' + (o.closed? ? '-closed' : '')}

74:   validates_presence_of :status, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.status_id_changed?}
75:   validates_presence_of :author, :if => Proc.new {|issue| issue.new_record? || issue.author_id_changed?}

79:   validates :estimated_hours, :numericality => {:greater_than_or_equal_to => 0, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid}
80:   validates :start_date, :date => true

82:   validate :validate_issue, :validate_required_fields, :validate_permissions

84:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
85:     joins(:project).
86:     where(Issue.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))
87:   end)
88: 
89:   scope :open, (lambda do |*args|
90:     is_closed = !args.empty? ? !args.first : false
91:     joins(:status).
92:     where(:issue_statuses => {:is_closed => is_closed})
93:   end)
94: 
95:   scope :recently_updated, lambda {order(:updated_on => :desc)}
96:   scope :on_active_project, (lambda do
97:     joins(:project).
98:     where(:projects => {:status => Project::STATUS_ACTIVE})
99:   end)
100:   scope :fixed_version, (lambda do |versions|
101:     ids = [versions].flatten.compact.map {|v| v.is_a?(Version) ? v.id : v}
102:     ids.any? ? where(:fixed_version_id => ids) : none
103:   end)
104:   scope :assigned_to, (lambda do |arg|
105:     arg = Array(arg).uniq
106:     ids = arg.map {|p| p.is_a?(Principal) ? p.id : p}
107:     ids += arg.grep(User).map(&:group_ids).flatten.uniq
108:     ids.compact!
109:     ids.any? ? where(:assigned_to_id => ids) : none
110:   end)
111:   scope :like, (lambda do |q|
112:     if q.present?
113:       where(*::Query.tokenized_like_conditions("#{table_name}.subject", q))
114:     end
115:   end)

132:   after_create_commit :send_notification

136:   # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
137:   def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
138:     Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
139:       sql =
140:         if user.id && user.logged?
141:           case role.issues_visibility
142:           when 'all'
143:             '1=1'
144:           when 'default'
145:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
146:             "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
147:               "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
148:               "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
149:           when 'own'
150:             user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
151:             "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
152:               "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
153:           else
154:             '1=0'
155:           end
156:         else
157:           "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
158:         end
159:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
160:         tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
161:         if tracker_ids.any?
162:           sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
163:         else
164:           sql = '1=0'
165:         end
166:       end
167:       sql
168:     end
169:   end

171:   # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
172:   def visible?(usr=nil)
173:     (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
174:       visible =
175:         if user.logged?
176:           case role.issues_visibility
177:           when 'all'
178:             true
179:           when 'default'
180:             !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
181:           when 'own'
182:             self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
183:           else
184:             false
185:           end
186:         else
187:           !self.is_private?
188:         end
189:       unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
190:         visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
191:       end
192:       visible
193:     end
194:   end
195: 
196:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
197:   def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
198:     user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
199:   end
200: 
201:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit or add notes to the issue
202:   def editable?(user=User.current)
203:     attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
204:   end
205: 
206:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit the issue
207:   def attributes_editable?(user=User.current)
208:     user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_issues) || (
209:       user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_own_issues) && author == user
210:     )
211:   end
212: 
213:   def attachments_addable?(user=User.current)
214:     attributes_editable?(user) || notes_addable?(user)
215:   end
216: 
217:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_editable?
218:   def attachments_editable?(user=User.current)
219:     visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
220:   end
221: 
222:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to add notes to the issue
223:   def notes_addable?(user=User.current)
224:     user_tracker_permission?(user, :add_issue_notes)
225:   end
226: 
227:   # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to delete the issue
228:   def deletable?(user=User.current)
229:     user_tracker_permission?(user, :delete_issues)
230:   end
231: 
232:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Attachable::InstanceMethods#attachments_deletable?
233:   def attachments_deletable?(user=User.current)
234:     visible?(user) && attributes_editable?(user)
235:   end

237:   def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
238:     super
239:     if new_record?
240:       # set default values for new records only
241:       self.priority ||= IssuePriority.default
242:       self.watcher_user_ids = []

246:   def create_or_update(*args)
247:     super()
248:   ensure
249:     @status_was = nil
250:   end
251:   private :create_or_update
252: 
253:   # AR#Persistence#destroy would raise and RecordNotFound exception
254:   # if the issue was already deleted or updated (non matching lock_version).
255:   # This is a problem when bulk deleting issues or deleting a project
256:   # (because an issue may already be deleted if its parent was deleted
257:   # first).
258:   # The issue is reloaded by the nested_set before being deleted so
259:   # the lock_version condition should not be an issue but we handle it.
260:   def destroy
261:     super
262:   rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError, ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
263:     # Stale or already deleted
264:     begin
265:       reload
266:     rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
267:       # The issue was actually already deleted
268:       @destroyed = true
269:       return freeze
270:     end
271:     # The issue was stale, retry to destroy
272:     super
273:   end

275:   alias :base_reload :reload
276:   def reload(*)
277:     @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
278:     @assignable_versions = nil
279:     @relations = nil
280:     @spent_hours = nil
281:     @total_spent_hours = nil

305:   # Copies attributes from another issue, arg can be an id or an Issue
306:   def copy_from(arg, options={})
307:     issue = arg.is_a?(Issue) ? arg : Issue.visible.find(arg)
308:     self.attributes =
309:       issue.attributes.dup.except(
310:         "id", "root_id", "parent_id", "lft", "rgt",
311:         "created_on", "updated_on", "status_id", "closed_on"
312:       )
313:     self.custom_field_values =
314:       issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
315:         [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
316:       end
317:     if options[:keep_status]
318:       self.status = issue.status
319:     end
320:     self.author = User.current
321:     unless options[:attachments] == false
322:       self.attachments = issue.attachments.map do |attachement|
323:         attachement.copy(:container => self)
324:       end
325:     end
326: 
327:     unless options[:watchers] == false
328:       self.watcher_user_ids = issue.visible_watcher_users.select{|u| u.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE}.map(&:id)
329:     end
330:     @copied_from = issue
331:     @copy_options = options
332:     self
333:   end

335:   # Returns an unsaved copy of the issue
336:   def copy(attributes=nil, copy_options={})
337:     copy = self.class.new.copy_from(self, copy_options)
338:     copy.attributes = attributes if attributes
339:     copy
340:   end

384:   # Sets the tracker.
385:   # This will set the status to the default status of the new tracker if:
386:   # * the status was the default for the previous tracker
387:   # * or if the status was not part of the new tracker statuses
388:   # * or the status was nil
389:   def tracker=(tracker)
390:     tracker_was = self.tracker
391:     association(:tracker).writer(tracker)
392:     if tracker != tracker_was
393:       if status == tracker_was.try(:default_status)
394:         self.status = nil
395:       elsif status && tracker && !tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status.id)
396:         self.status = nil
397:       end
398:       reassign_custom_field_values
399:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
400:     end
401:     self.status ||= default_status
402:     self.tracker
403:   end

405:   def project_id=(project_id)
406:     if project_id.to_s != self.project_id.to_s
407:       self.project = (project_id.present? ? Project.find_by_id(project_id) : nil)
408:     end
409:     self.project_id
410:   end

412:   # Sets the project.
413:   # Unless keep_tracker argument is set to true, this will change the tracker
414:   # to the first tracker of the new project if the previous tracker is not part
415:   # of the new project trackers.
416:   # This will:
417:   # * clear the fixed_version is it's no longer valid for the new project.
418:   # * clear the parent issue if it's no longer valid for the new project.
419:   # * set the category to the category with the same name in the new
420:   #   project if it exists, or clear it if it doesn't.
421:   # * for new issue, set the fixed_version to the project default version
422:   #   if it's a valid fixed_version.
423:   def project=(project, keep_tracker=false)
424:     project_was = self.project
425:     association(:project).writer(project)
426:     if project != project_was
427:       @safe_attribute_names = nil
428:     end
429:     if project_was && project && project_was != project
430:       @assignable_versions = nil
431: 
432:       unless keep_tracker || project.trackers.include?(tracker)
433:         self.tracker = project.trackers.first
434:       end
435:       # Reassign to the category with same name if any
436:       if category
437:         self.category = project.issue_categories.find_by_name(category.name)
438:       end
439:       # Clear the assignee if not available in the new project for new issues (eg. copy)
440:       # For existing issue, the previous assignee is still valid, so we keep it
441:       if new_record? && assigned_to && !assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
442:         self.assigned_to_id = nil
443:       end
444:       # Keep the fixed_version if it's still valid in the new_project
445:       if fixed_version && fixed_version.project != project && !project.shared_versions.include?(fixed_version)
446:         self.fixed_version = nil
447:       end
448:       # Clear the parent task if it's no longer valid
449:       unless valid_parent_project?
450:         self.parent_issue_id = nil
451:       end
452:       reassign_custom_field_values
453:       @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
454:     end
455:     # Set fixed_version to the project default version if it's valid
456:     if new_record? && fixed_version.nil? && project && project.default_version_id?
457:       if project.shared_versions.open.exists?(project.default_version_id)
458:         self.fixed_version_id = project.default_version_id
459:       end
460:     end
461:     self.project
462:   end

541:   def safe_attribute_names(user=nil)
542:     names = super
543:     names -= disabled_core_fields
544:     names -= read_only_attribute_names(user)
545:     if new_record?
546:       # Make sure that project_id can always be set for new issues
547:       names |= %w(project_id)
548:     end
549:     if dates_derived?
550:       names -= %w(start_date due_date)
551:     end
552:     if priority_derived?
553:       names -= %w(priority_id)
554:     end
555:     if done_ratio_derived?
556:       names -= %w(done_ratio)
557:     end
558:     names
559:   end

561:   # Safely sets attributes
562:   # Should be called from controllers instead of #attributes=
563:   # attr_accessible is too rough because we still want things like
564:   # Issue.new(:project => foo) to work
565:   def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
566:     if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
567:       attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
568:     end
569: 
570:     @attributes_set_by = user
571:     return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
572: 
573:     attrs = attrs.deep_dup
574: 
575:     # Project and Tracker must be set before since new_statuses_allowed_to depends on it.
576:     if (p = attrs.delete('project_id')) && safe_attribute?('project_id')
577:       if p.is_a?(String) && !/^\d*$/.match?(p)
578:         p_id = Project.find_by_identifier(p).try(:id)
579:       else
580:         p_id = p.to_i
581:       end
582:       if allowed_target_projects(user).where(:id => p_id).exists?
583:         self.project_id = p_id
584:       end
585: 
586:       if project_id_changed? && attrs['category_id'].present? && attrs['category_id'].to_s == category_id_was.to_s
587:         # Discard submitted category on previous project
588:         attrs.delete('category_id')
589:       end
590:     end
591: 
592:     if (t = attrs.delete('tracker_id')) && safe_attribute?('tracker_id')
593:       if allowed_target_trackers(user).where(:id => t.to_i).exists?
594:         self.tracker_id = t
595:       end
596:     end
597:     if project && tracker.nil?
598:       # Set a default tracker to accept custom field values
599:       # even if tracker is not specified
600:       allowed_trackers = allowed_target_trackers(user)
601: 
602:       if attrs['parent_issue_id'].present?
603:         # If parent_issue_id is present, the first tracker for which this field
604:         # is not disabled is chosen as default
605:         self.tracker = allowed_trackers.detect {|t| t.core_fields.include?('parent_issue_id')}
606:       end
607:       self.tracker ||= allowed_trackers.first
608:     end
609: 
610:     statuses_allowed = new_statuses_allowed_to(user)
611:     if (s = attrs.delete('status_id')) && safe_attribute?('status_id')
612:       if statuses_allowed.collect(&:id).include?(s.to_i)
613:         self.status_id = s
614:       end
615:     end
616:     if new_record? && !statuses_allowed.include?(status)
617:       self.status = statuses_allowed.first || default_status
618:     end
619:     # Use the selected tracker's private default when the form has no explicit value.
620:     if new_record? && tracker&.private_by_default? &&
621:          !attrs.key?('is_private') && safe_attribute?('is_private', user)
622:       attrs['is_private'] = '1'
623:     end
624:     if (u = attrs.delete('assigned_to_id')) && safe_attribute?('assigned_to_id')
625:       self.assigned_to_id = u
626:     end
627:     attrs = delete_unsafe_attributes(attrs, user)
628:     return if attrs.empty?
629: 
630:     if attrs['parent_issue_id'].present?
631:       s = attrs['parent_issue_id'].to_s
632:       unless (m = s.match(%r{\A#?(\d+)\z})) && (m[1] == parent_id.to_s || Issue.visible(user).exists?(m[1]))
633:         @invalid_parent_issue_id = attrs.delete('parent_issue_id')
634:       end
635:     end
636: 
637:     if attrs['custom_field_values'].present?
638:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
639:       attrs['custom_field_values'].select! {|k, v| editable_custom_field_ids.include?(k.to_s)}
640:     end
641: 
642:     if attrs['custom_fields'].present?
643:       editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
644:       attrs['custom_fields'].select! {|c| editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c['id'].to_s)}
645:     end
646: 
647:     assign_attributes attrs
648:   end
649: 
650:   def disabled_core_fields
651:     tracker ? tracker.disabled_core_fields : []
652:   end
653: 
654:   # Returns the custom_field_values that can be edited by the given user
655:   def editable_custom_field_values(user=nil)
656:     read_only = read_only_attribute_names(user)
657:     visible_custom_field_values(user).reject do |value|
658:       read_only.include?(value.custom_field_id.to_s)
659:     end
660:   end
661: 
662:   # Returns the custom fields that can be edited by the given user
663:   def editable_custom_fields(user=nil)
664:     editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field).uniq
665:   end
666: 
667:   # Returns the names of attributes that are read-only for user or the current user
668:   # For users with multiple roles, the read-only fields are the intersection of
669:   # read-only fields of each role
670:   # The result is an array of strings where sustom fields are represented with their ids
671:   #
672:   # Examples:
673:   #   issue.read_only_attribute_names # => ['due_date', '2']
674:   #   issue.read_only_attribute_names(user) # => []
675:   def read_only_attribute_names(user=nil)
676:     workflow_rule_by_attribute(user).reject {|attr, rule| rule != 'readonly'}.keys
677:   end
678: 
679:   # Returns the names of required attributes for user or the current user
680:   # For users with multiple roles, the required fields are the intersection of
681:   # required fields of each role
682:   # The result is an array of strings where sustom fields are represented with their ids
683:   #
684:   # Examples:
685:   #   issue.required_attribute_names # => ['due_date', '2']
686:   #   issue.required_attribute_names(user) # => []
687:   def required_attribute_names(user=nil)
688:     workflow_rule_by_attribute(user).reject {|attr, rule| rule != 'required'}.keys
689:   end
690: 
691:   # Returns true if the attribute is required for user
692:   def required_attribute?(name, user=nil)
693:     required_attribute_names(user).include?(name.to_s)
694:   end
695: 
696:   # Returns a hash of the workflow rule by attribute for the given user
697:   #
698:   # Examples:
699:   #   issue.workflow_rule_by_attribute # => {'due_date' => 'required', 'start_date' => 'readonly'}
700:   def workflow_rule_by_attribute(user=nil)
701:     return @workflow_rule_by_attribute if @workflow_rule_by_attribute && user.nil?
702: 
703:     roles = roles_for_workflow(user || User.current)
704:     return {} if roles.empty?
705: 
706:     result = {}
707:     workflow_permissions =
708:       WorkflowPermission.where(
709:         :tracker_id => tracker_id, :old_status_id => status_id,
710:         :role_id => roles.map(&:id)
711:       ).to_a
712:     if workflow_permissions.any?
713:       workflow_rules = workflow_permissions.inject({}) do |h, wp|
714:         h[wp.field_name] ||= {}
715:         h[wp.field_name][wp.role_id] = wp.rule
716:         h
717:       end
718:       fields_with_roles = {}
719:       IssueCustomField.where(:visible => false).
720:         joins(:roles).pluck(:id, "role_id").
721:           each do |field_id, role_id|
722:         fields_with_roles[field_id] ||= []
723:         fields_with_roles[field_id] << role_id
724:       end
725:       roles.each do |role|
726:         fields_with_roles.each do |field_id, role_ids|
727:           unless role_ids.include?(role.id)
728:             field_name = field_id.to_s
729:             workflow_rules[field_name] ||= {}
730:             workflow_rules[field_name][role.id] = 'readonly'
731:           end
732:         end
733:       end
734:       workflow_rules.each do |attr, rules|
735:         next if rules.size < roles.size
736: 
737:         uniq_rules = rules.values.uniq
738:         if uniq_rules.size == 1
739:           result[attr] = uniq_rules.first
740:         else
741:           result[attr] = 'required'
742:         end
743:       end
744:     end
745:     @workflow_rule_by_attribute = result if user.nil?
746:     result
747:   end
748:   private :workflow_rule_by_attribute

750:   def done_ratio
751:     if Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && status && status.default_done_ratio
752:       status.default_done_ratio
753:     else
754:       read_attribute(:done_ratio)
755:     end
756:   end

758:   def self.use_status_for_done_ratio?
759:     Setting.issue_done_ratio == 'issue_status'
760:   end
761: 
762:   def self.use_field_for_done_ratio?
763:     Setting.issue_done_ratio == 'issue_field'
764:   end

766:   def validate_issue
767:     if due_date && start_date && (start_date_changed? || due_date_changed?) && due_date < start_date
768:       errors.add :due_date, :greater_than_start_date
769:     end
770: 
771:     if start_date && start_date_changed? && soonest_start && start_date < soonest_start
772:       errors.add :start_date, :earlier_than_minimum_start_date, :date => format_date(soonest_start)
773:     end
774: 
775:     if project && fixed_version_id
776:       if fixed_version.nil? || assignable_versions.exclude?(fixed_version)
777:         errors.add :fixed_version_id, :inclusion
778:       elsif reopening? && fixed_version.closed?
779:         errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_can_not_reopen_issue_on_closed_version)
780:       end
781:     end
782: 
783:     if project && category_id
784:       unless project.issue_category_ids.include?(category_id)
785:         errors.add :category_id, :inclusion
786:       end
787:     end
788: 
789:     # Checks that the issue can not be added/moved to a disabled tracker
790:     if project && (tracker_id_changed? || project_id_changed?)
791:       if tracker && !project.trackers.include?(tracker)
792:         errors.add :tracker_id, :inclusion
793:       end
794:     end
795: 
796:     if project && assigned_to_id_changed? && assigned_to_id.present?
797:       unless assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
798:         errors.add :assigned_to_id, :invalid
799:       end
800:     end
801: 
802:     # Checks parent issue assignment
803:     if @invalid_parent_issue_id.present?
804:       errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
805:     elsif @parent_issue
806:       if !valid_parent_project?(@parent_issue)
807:         errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
808:       elsif (@parent_issue != parent) && (
809:           self.would_reschedule?(@parent_issue) ||
810:           @parent_issue.self_and_ancestors.any? do |a|
811:             a.relations_from.any? do |r|
812:               r.relation_type == IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES &&
813:                  r.issue_to.would_reschedule?(self)
814:             end
815:           end
816:         )
817:         errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
818:       elsif !closed? && @parent_issue.closed?
819:         # cannot attach an open issue to a closed parent
820:         errors.add :base, :open_issue_with_closed_parent
821:       elsif !new_record?
822:         # moving an existing issue
823:         if move_possible?(@parent_issue)
824:           # move accepted
825:         else
826:           errors.add :parent_issue_id, :invalid
827:         end
828:       end
829:     end
830:   end
831: 
832:   # Validates the issue against additional workflow requirements
833:   def validate_required_fields
834:     user = new_record? ? author : current_journal.try(:user)
835: 
836:     required_attribute_names(user).each do |attribute|
837:       if /^\d+$/.match?(attribute)
838:         attribute = attribute.to_i
839:         v = custom_field_values.detect {|v| v.custom_field_id == attribute}
840:         if v && Array(v.value).detect(&:present?).nil?
841:           errors.add(v.custom_field.name, l('activerecord.errors.messages.blank'))
842:         end
843:       else
844:         if respond_to?(attribute) && send(attribute).blank? && !disabled_core_fields.include?(attribute)
845:           next if attribute == 'category_id' && project.try(:issue_categories).blank?
846:           next if attribute == 'fixed_version_id' && assignable_versions.blank?
847: 
848:           errors.add attribute, :blank
849:         end
850:       end
851:     end
852:   end
853: 
854:   def validate_permissions
855:     if @attributes_set_by && new_record? && copy?
856:       unless allowed_target_trackers(@attributes_set_by).include?(tracker)
857:         errors.add :tracker, :invalid
858:       end
859:     end
860:   end

862:   # Overrides Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods#validate_custom_field_values
863:   # so that custom values that are not editable are not validated (eg. a custom field that
864:   # is marked as required should not trigger a validation error if the user is not allowed
865:   # to edit this field).
866:   def validate_custom_field_values
867:     user = new_record? ? author : current_journal.try(:user)
868:     if new_record? || custom_field_values_changed?
869:       editable_custom_field_values(user).each(&:validate_value)
870:     end
871:   end

873:   # Set the done_ratio using the status if that setting is set.  This will keep the done_ratios

1084:   # Returns an array of statuses that user is able to apply
1085:   def new_statuses_allowed_to(user=User.current, include_default=false)
1086:     initial_status = nil
1087:     if new_record?
1088:       # nop
1089:     elsif tracker_id_changed?
1090:       if Tracker.where(:id => tracker_id_was, :default_status_id => status_id_was).any?
1091:         initial_status = default_status
1092:       elsif tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status_id_was)
1093:         initial_status = IssueStatus.find_by_id(status_id_was)
1094:       else
1095:         initial_status = default_status
1096:       end
1097:     else
1098:       initial_status = status_was
1099:     end
1100: 
1101:     initial_assigned_to_id = assigned_to_id_changed? ? assigned_to_id_was : assigned_to_id
1102:     assignee_transitions_allowed = initial_assigned_to_id.present? &&
1103:       (user.id == initial_assigned_to_id || user.group_ids.include?(initial_assigned_to_id))
1104: 
1105:     statuses = []
1106:     statuses += IssueStatus.new_statuses_allowed(
1107:       initial_status,
1108:       roles_for_workflow(user),
1109:       tracker,
1110:       author == user,
1111:       assignee_transitions_allowed
1112:     )
1113:     statuses << initial_status unless statuses.empty?
1114:     statuses << default_status if include_default || (new_record? && statuses.empty?)
1115: 
1116:     statuses = statuses.compact.uniq.sort
1117:     unless closable?
1118:       # cannot close a blocked issue or a parent with open subtasks
1119:       statuses.reject!(&:is_closed?)
1120:     end
1121:     unless reopenable?
1122:       # cannot reopen a subtask of a closed parent
1123:       statuses.select!(&:is_closed?)
1124:     end
1125:     statuses
1126:   end

1233:           :@relations,

1244:       issues.each do |issue|

1254:         joins("JOIN #{Issue.table_name} parent ON parent.root_id = #{Issue.table_name}.root_id" +
1255:           " AND parent.lft <= #{Issue.table_name}.lft AND parent.rgt >= #{Issue.table_name}.rgt").
1256:         where("parent.id IN (?)", issues.map(&:id)).group("parent.id").sum(:hours)
1257:       issues.each do |issue|

1263:   # Preloads visible relations for a collection of issues
1264:   def self.load_visible_relations(issues, user=User.current)
1265:     if issues.any?
1266:       issue_ids = issues.map(&:id)
1267:       # Relations with issue_from in given issues and visible issue_to
1268:       relations_from = IssueRelation.joins(:issue_to => :project).
1269:                          where(visible_condition(user)).where(:issue_from_id => issue_ids).to_a
1270:       # Relations with issue_to in given issues and visible issue_from
1271:       relations_to = IssueRelation.joins(:issue_from => :project).
1272:                          where(visible_condition(user)).
1273:                          where(:issue_to_id => issue_ids).to_a
1274:       issues.each do |issue|
1275:         relations =
1276:           relations_from.select {|relation| relation.issue_from_id == issue.id} +
1277:           relations_to.select {|relation| relation.issue_to_id == issue.id}
1278: 
1279:         issue.instance_variable_set :@relations, IssueRelation::Relations.new(issue, relations.sort)
1280:       end
1281:     end

1338:   # Returns true if this issue blocks the other issue, otherwise returns false
1339:   def blocks?(other)
1340:     all = [self]
1341:     last = [self]
1342:     while last.any?
1343:       current =
1344:         last.map do |i|
1345:           i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_BLOCKS).map(&:issue_to)
1346:         end.flatten.uniq
1347:       current -= last
1348:       current -= all
1349:       return true if current.include?(other)
1350: 
1351:       last = current
1352:       all += last
1353:     end
1354:     false
1355:   end
1356: 
1357:   # Returns true if the other issue might be rescheduled if the start/due dates of this issue change
1358:   def would_reschedule?(other)
1359:     all = [self]
1360:     last = [self]
1361:     while last.any?
1362:       current = last.map do |i|
1363:         i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to) +
1364:         i.leaves.to_a +
1365:         i.ancestors.map {|a| a.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to)}
1366:       end.flatten.uniq
1367:       current -= last
1368:       current -= all
1369:       return true if current.include?(other)
1370: 
1371:       last = current
1372:       all += last
1373:     end
1374:     false
1375:   end

1397:   # Returns the duration in working days

1399:     (start_date && due_date) ? working_days(start_date, due_date) : 0
1400:   end

1402:   def soonest_start(reload=false)
1403:     if @soonest_start.nil? || reload
1404:       relations_to.reload if reload
1405:       dates = relations_to.collect{|relation| relation.successor_soonest_start}
1406:       p = @parent_issue || parent
1407:       if p && Setting.parent_issue_dates == 'derived'
1408:         dates << p.soonest_start
1409:       end
1410:       @soonest_start = dates.compact.max
1411:     end
1412:     @soonest_start
1413:   end

1415:   # Sets start_date on the given date or the next working day
1416:   # and changes due_date to keep the same working duration.
1417:   def reschedule_on(date)
1418:     wd = working_duration
1419:     date = next_working_date(date)
1420:     self.start_date = date
1421:     self.due_date = add_working_days(date, wd)
1422:   end
1423: 
1424:   # Reschedules the issue on the given date or the next working day and saves the record.
1425:   # If the issue is a parent task, this is done by rescheduling its subtasks.
1426:   def reschedule_on!(date, journal=nil)
1427:     return if date.nil?
1428: 
1429:     if leaf? || !dates_derived?
1430:       if start_date.nil? || start_date != date
1431:         if start_date && start_date > date
1432:           # Issue can not be moved earlier than its soonest start date
1433:           date = [soonest_start(true), date].compact.max
1434:         end
1435:         if journal
1436:           init_journal(journal.user)
1437:         end
1438:         reschedule_on(date)
1439:         begin
1440:           save
1441:         rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
1442:           reload
1443:           reschedule_on(date)
1444:           save
1445:         end
1446:       end
1447:     else
1448:       leaves.each do |leaf|
1449:         if leaf.start_date
1450:           # Only move subtask if it starts at the same date as the parent
1451:           # or if it starts before the given date
1452:           if start_date == leaf.start_date || date > leaf.start_date
1453:             leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
1454:           end
1455:         else
1456:           leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
1457:         end
1458:       end
1459:     end
1460:   end
1461: 
1462:   def dates_derived?
1463:     !leaf? && Setting.parent_issue_dates == 'derived'
1464:   end
1465: 
1466:   def priority_derived?
1467:     !leaf? && Setting.parent_issue_priority == 'derived'
1468:   end
1469: 
1470:   def done_ratio_derived?
1471:     !leaf? && Setting.parent_issue_done_ratio == 'derived'
1472:   end

1474:   def <=>(issue)
1475:     return nil unless issue.is_a?(Issue)
1476: 
1477:     if root_id != issue.root_id
1478:       (root_id || 0) <=> (issue.root_id || 0)
1479:     else
1480:       (lft || 0) <=> (issue.lft || 0)
1481:     end
1482:   end

1628:   # Query generator for selecting groups of issue counts for a project
1629:   # based on specific criteria
1630:   #
1631:   # Options
1632:   # * project - Project to search in.
1633:   # * with_subprojects - Includes subprojects issues if set to true.
1634:   # * association - Symbol. Association for grouping.
1635:   def self.count_and_group_by(options)
1636:     assoc = reflect_on_association(options[:association])
1637:     select_field = assoc.foreign_key
1638: 
1639:     Issue.
1640:       visible(User.current, :project => options[:project], :with_subprojects => options[:with_subprojects]).
1641:       joins(:status).
1642:       group(:status_id, :is_closed, select_field).
1643:       count.
1644:       map do |columns, total|
1645:         status_id, is_closed, field_value = columns
1646:         is_closed = ['t', 'true', '1'].include?(is_closed.to_s)
1647:         {
1648:           "status_id" => status_id.to_s,
1649:           "closed" => is_closed,
1650:           select_field => field_value.to_s,
1651:           "total" => total.to_s
1652:         }
1653:       end
1654:   end

1697:     if project
1698:       scope = project.trackers.sorted
1699:       unless user.admin?
1700:         roles = user.roles_for_project(project).select {|r| r.has_permission?(:add_issues)}
1701:         unless roles.any? {|r| r.permissions_all_trackers?(:add_issues)}
1702:           tracker_ids = roles.map {|r| r.permissions_tracker_ids(:add_issues)}.flatten.uniq
1703:           if current_tracker
1704:             tracker_ids << current_tracker
1705:           end
1706:           scope = scope.where(:id => tracker_ids)
1707:         end

1709:       scope

1711:       Tracker.none

1737: 
1738:     # Delete issue relations

1740:       relations_from.clear
1741:       relations_to.clear

1743: 
1744:     # Move subtasks that were in the same project
1745:     children.each do |child|
1746:       next unless child.project_id == project_id_before_last_save
1747: 
1748:       # Change project and keep project
1749:       child.send :project=, project, true
1750:       unless child.save
1751:         errors.add(
1752:           :base,
1753:           l(:error_move_of_child_not_possible,
1754:             :child => "##{child.id}",

1762:   # Callback for after the creation of an issue by copy
1763:   # * adds a "copied to" relation with the copied issue
1764:   # * copies subtasks from the copied issue
1765:   def after_create_from_copy
1766:     return unless copy? && !@after_create_from_copy_handled
1767: 
1768:     if (@copied_from.project_id == project_id ||
1769:           Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?) &&
1770:         @copy_options[:link] != false
1771:       if @current_journal
1772:         @copied_from.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
1773:       end
1774:       relation =
1775:         IssueRelation.new(:issue_from => @copied_from, :issue_to => self,
1776:                           :relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_COPIED_TO)
1777:       unless relation.save
1778:         if logger
1779:           logger.error(
1780:             "Could not create relation while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} " \
1781:               "due to validation errors: #{relation.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
1782:           )
1783:         end
1784:       end
1785:     end
1786: 
1787:     unless @copied_from.leaf? || @copy_options[:subtasks] == false
1788:       copy_options = (@copy_options || {}).merge(:subtasks => false)
1789:       copied_issue_ids = {@copied_from.id => self.id}
1790:       @copied_from.reload.descendants.reorder("#{Issue.table_name}.lft").each do |child|
1791:         # Do not copy self when copying an issue as a descendant of the copied issue
1792:         next if child == self
1793:         # Do not copy subtasks of issues that were not copied
1794:         next unless copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
1795: 
1796:         # Do not copy subtasks that are not visible to avoid potential disclosure of private data
1797:         unless child.visible?
1798:           if logger
1799:             logger.error(
1800:               "Subtask ##{child.id} was not copied during ##{@copied_from.id} copy " \
1801:                 "because it is not visible to the current user"
1802:             )
1803:           end
1804:           next
1805:         end
1806:         copy = Issue.new.copy_from(child, copy_options)
1807:         if @current_journal
1808:           copy.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
1809:         end
1810:         copy.author = author
1811:         copy.project = project
1812:         copy.parent_issue_id = copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
1813:         unless child.fixed_version.present? && child.fixed_version.status == 'open'
1814:           copy.fixed_version_id = nil
1815:         end
1816:         unless child.assigned_to_id.present? &&
1817:                  child.assigned_to.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE
1818:           copy.assigned_to = nil
1819:         end
1820:         unless copy.save
1821:           if logger
1822:             logger.error(
1823:               "Could not copy subtask ##{child.id} " \
1824:                 "while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} due to validation errors: " \
1825:                 "#{copy.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
1826:             )
1827:           end
1828:           next
1829:         end
1830:         copied_issue_ids[child.id] = copy.id
1831:       end
1832:     end
1833:     @after_create_from_copy_handled = true
1834:   end

1863:   def recalculate_attributes_for(issue_id)
1864:     if issue_id && p = Issue.find_by_id(issue_id)
1865:       if p.priority_derived?
1866:         # priority = highest priority of open children
1867:         # priority is left unchanged if all children are closed and there's no default priority defined
1868:         if priority_position =
1869:              p.children.open.joins(:priority).maximum("#{IssuePriority.table_name}.position")
1870:           p.priority = IssuePriority.find_by_position(priority_position)
1871:         elsif default_priority = IssuePriority.default
1872:           p.priority = default_priority
1873:         end
1874:       end
1875: 
1876:       if p.dates_derived?
1877:         # start/due dates = lowest/highest dates of children
1878:         p.start_date = p.children.minimum(:start_date)
1879:         p.due_date = p.children.maximum(:due_date)
1880:         if p.start_date && p.due_date && p.due_date < p.start_date
1881:           p.start_date, p.due_date = p.due_date, p.start_date
1882:         end
1883:       end
1884: 
1885:       if p.done_ratio_derived?
1886:         # done ratio = average ratio of children weighted with their total estimated hours
1887:         unless Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && p.status && p.status.default_done_ratio
1888:           children = p.children.to_a
1889:           if children.any?
1890:             child_with_total_estimated_hours = children.select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
1891:             if child_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
1892:               average = Rational(
1893:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_s,
1894:                 child_with_total_estimated_hours.count
1895:               )
1896:             else
1897:               average = Rational(1)
1898:             end
1899:             done = children.sum do |c|
1900:               estimated = Rational(c.total_estimated_hours.to_f.to_s)
1901:               estimated = average unless estimated > 0.0
1902:               ratio = c.closed? ? 100 : (c.done_ratio || 0)
1903:               estimated * ratio
1904:             end
1905:             progress = Rational(done, average * children.count)
1906:             p.done_ratio = progress.floor
1907:           end
1908:         end
1909:       end
1910: 
1911:       # ancestors will be recursively updated
1912:       p.save(:validate => false)
1913:     end
1914:   end

1918:     # Update issues so their versions are not pointing to a

1921:       # Only need to update issues with a fixed_version from
1922:       # a different project and that is not systemwide shared
1923:       Issue.joins(:project, :fixed_version).
1924:         where("#{Issue.table_name}.fixed_version_id IS NOT NULL" +
1925:           " AND #{Issue.table_name}.project_id <> #{::Version.table_name}.project_id" +
1926:           " AND #{::Version.table_name}.sharing <> 'system'").
1927:         where(conditions).each do |issue|
1928:         next if issue.project.nil? || issue.fixed_version.nil?
1929: 
1930:         unless issue.project.shared_versions.include?(issue.fixed_version)
1931:           retried = false
1932:           begin
1933:             issue.init_journal(User.current)
1934:             issue.fixed_version = nil
1935:             issue.save
1936:           rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
1937:             raise if retried
1938: 
1939:             retried = true
1940:             issue.reload
1941:             retry

2064:         [parent_id, nil]
2065:       else
2066:         [nil, parent_id]
2067:       end
2068: 
2069:     if old_parent_id.present?
2070:       Issue.transaction do
2071:         if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
2072:           old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
2073:           old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
2074:           old_parent_issue.save
2075:         end
2076:       end
2077:     end
2078: 
2079:     if new_parent_id.present?
2080:       Issue.transaction do
2081:         if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
2082:           new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
2083:           new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
2084:           new_parent_issue.save
2085:         end
2086:       end
2087:     end
2088:   end
2089: 
2090:   def add_auto_watcher
2091:     if author&.active? &&
2092:         author.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, project) &&
2093:         author.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_created') &&
2094:         self.watcher_user_ids.exclude?(author.id)
2095:       self.set_watcher(author, true)
2096:     end
2097:   end
2098: 
2099:   def send_notification
2100:     if notify? && Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_added')
2101:       Mailer.deliver_issue_add(self)
2102:     end
2103:   end
2104: 
2105:   def clear_disabled_fields
2106:     if tracker
2107:       tracker.disabled_core_fields.each do |attribute|
2108:         send :"#{attribute}=", nil
2109:       end
2110:       self.priority_id ||= IssuePriority.default&.id || IssuePriority.active.first&.id
2111:       self.done_ratio ||= 0
2112:     end
2113:   end
2114: 
2115:   # Forcefully set the default value to any custom field values which are not
2116:   # editable by the current user when creating a new issue. This may overwrite
2117:   # existing custom values of a copied issue which are not editable by the
2118:   # current user.
2119:   def force_default_value_on_noneditable_custom_fields
2120:     return unless custom_field_values_changed?
2121: 
2122:     editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_fields(author).map(&:id)
2123:     custom_field_values.each do |field_value|
2124:       unless editable_custom_field_ids.include?(field_value.custom_field_id)
2125:         field_value.value = field_value.custom_field.default_value
2126:       end
2127:     end
2128:   end
2129: 
2130:   def filter_projects_scope(scope=nil)
2131:     case scope
2132:     when 'system'
2133:       Project
2134:     when 'tree'
2135:       project.root.self_and_descendants
2136:     when 'hierarchy'
2137:       project.hierarchy
2138:     when 'descendants'
2139:       project.self_and_descendants
2140:     when ''
2141:       Project.where(:id => project.id)
2142:     else
2143:       Project
2144:     end
2145:   end
2146: 
2147:   def roles_for_workflow(user)
2148:     roles = user.admin ? Role.all.to_a : user.roles_for_project(project)
2149:     roles.select(&:consider_workflow?)
2150:   end
2151: end

45:   def index
46:     use_session = !request.format.csv?
47:     retrieve_default_query(use_session)
48:     retrieve_query(IssueQuery, use_session)
49: 
50:     if @query.valid?
51:       respond_to do |format|
52:         format.html do
53:           @issue_count = @query.issue_count
54:           @issue_pages = Paginator.new @issue_count, per_page_option, params['page']
55:           @issues = @query.issues(:offset => @issue_pages.offset, :limit => @issue_pages.per_page)
56:           render :layout => !request.xhr?
57:         end
58:         format.api do
59:           @offset, @limit = api_offset_and_limit
60:           @query.column_names = %w(author)
61:           @issue_count = @query.issue_count
62:           @issues = @query.issues(:offset => @offset, :limit => @limit)
63:           Issue.load_visible_relations(@issues) if include_in_api_response?('relations')
64:           if User.current.allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, nil, :global => true)
65:             Issue.load_visible_spent_hours(@issues)
66:             Issue.load_visible_total_spent_hours(@issues)
67:           end
68:         end
69:         format.atom do
70:           issues = @query.issues(:limit => Setting.feeds_limit.to_i)
71:           render_feed(issues,
72:                       :title => "#{@project || Setting.app_title}: #{l(:label_issue_plural)}")
73:         end
74:         format.csv do
75:           issues = @query.issues(:limit => Setting.issues_export_limit.to_i)
76:           send_data(query_to_csv(issues, @query, params[:csv]),
77:                     :type => 'text/csv; header=present', :filename => "#{filename_for_export(@query, 'issues')}.csv")
78:         end
79:         format.pdf do
80:           @issues = @query.issues(:limit => Setting.issues_export_limit.to_i)
81:           send_file_headers! :type => 'application/pdf', :filename => "#{filename_for_export(@query, 'issues')}.pdf"
82:         end
83:       end
84:     else
85:       respond_to do |format|
86:         format.html {render :layout => !request.xhr?}
87:         format.any(:atom, :csv, :pdf) {head :unprocessable_content}
88:         format.api {render_validation_errors(@query)}
89:       end
90:     end
91:   rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
92:     render_404
93:   end

95:   def show
96:     if !api_request? || include_in_api_response?('journals')
97:       @journals = @issue.visible_journals_with_index
98:       @journals.reverse! if User.current.wants_comments_in_reverse_order?
99:     end
100:     if !api_request? || include_in_api_response?('relations')
101:       @relations = @issue.relations.select {|r| r.other_issue(@issue)&.visible?}
102:     end
103:     if !api_request? || include_in_api_response?('allowed_statuses')
104:       @allowed_statuses = @issue.new_statuses_allowed_to(User.current)
105:     end
106: 
107:     if User.current.allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, @project)
108:       Issue.load_visible_spent_hours([@issue])
109:       Issue.load_visible_total_spent_hours([@issue])
110:     end
111: 
112:     respond_to do |format|
113:       format.html do
114:         @priorities = IssuePriority.active
115:         if @project.module_enabled?(:time_tracking)
116:           @time_entry = TimeEntry.new(:issue => @issue, :project => @issue.project)
117:           @time_entries = @issue.time_entries.visible.preload(:activity, :user)
118:         else
119:           @time_entry = nil
120:           @time_entries = []
121:         end
122:         @relation = IssueRelation.new
123:         @has_changesets = @issue.changesets.visible.preload(:repository, :user).exists?
124:         retrieve_previous_and_next_issue_ids
125:         render :template => 'issues/show'
126:       end
127:       format.api do
128:         if include_in_api_response?('changesets')
129:           @changesets = @issue.changesets.visible.preload(:repository, :user).to_a
130:           @changesets.reverse! if User.current.wants_comments_in_reverse_order?
131:         end
132:       end
133:       format.atom do
134:         render :template => 'journals/index', :layout => false,
135:         :content_type => 'application/atom+xml'
136:       end
137:       format.pdf do
138:         send_file_headers!(:type => 'application/pdf',
139:                            :filename => "#{@project.identifier}-#{@issue.id}.pdf")
140:       end
141:     end
142:   end

144:   def new
145:     respond_to do |format|
146:       format.html {render :action => 'new', :layout => !request.xhr?}
147:       format.js
148:     end
149:   end

151:   def create
152:     unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issues, @issue.project, :global => true)
153:       raise ::Unauthorized
154:     end
155: 
156:     call_hook(:controller_issues_new_before_save, {:params => params, :issue => @issue})
157:     @issue.save_attachments(params[:attachments] || (params[:issue] && params[:issue][:uploads]))
158:     if @issue.save
159:       call_hook(:controller_issues_new_after_save, {:params => params, :issue => @issue})
160:       respond_to do |format|
161:         format.html do
162:           render_attachment_warning_if_needed(@issue)
163:           flash[:notice] =
164:             l(:notice_issue_successful_create,
165:               :id => view_context.link_to("##{@issue.id}", issue_path(@issue),
166:                                           :title => @issue.subject))
167:           redirect_after_create
168:         end
169:         format.api do
170:           render :action => 'show', :status => :created,
171:           :location => issue_url(@issue)
172:         end
173:       end
174:       return
175:     else
176:       respond_to do |format|
177:         format.html do
178:           if @issue.project.nil?
179:             render_error :status => 422
180:           else
181:             render :action => 'new'
182:           end
183:         end
184:         format.api  {render_validation_errors(@issue)}
185:       end
186:     end
187:   end
188: 
189:   def edit
190:     return unless update_issue_from_params
191: 
192:     respond_to do |format|
193:       format.html {}
194:       format.js
195:     end
196:   end
197: 
198:   def update
199:     return unless update_issue_from_params
200: 
201:     attachments = params[:attachments] || params.dig(:issue, :uploads)
202:     if @issue.attachments_addable?
203:       @issue.save_attachments(attachments)
204:     else
205:       attachments = attachments.to_unsafe_hash if attachments.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
206:       if [Hash, Array].any? { |klass| attachments.is_a?(klass) } && attachments.any?
207:         flash[:warning] = l(:warning_attachments_not_saved, attachments.size)
208:       end
209:     end
210: 
211:     saved = false
212:     begin
213:       saved = save_issue_with_child_records
214:     rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
215:       @issue.detach_saved_attachments
216:       @conflict = true
217:       if params[:last_journal_id]
218:         @conflict_journals = @issue.journals_after(params[:last_journal_id]).to_a
219:         unless User.current.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, @issue.project)
220:           @conflict_journals.reject!(&:private_notes?)
221:         end
222:       end
223:     end
224: 
225:     if saved
226:       render_attachment_warning_if_needed(@issue)
227:       unless @issue.current_journal.new_record? || params[:no_flash]
228:         flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update)
229:       end
230:       respond_to do |format|
231:         format.html do
232:           redirect_back_or_default issue_path(@issue),
233:             flash: { previous_and_next_issue_ids: previous_and_next_issue_ids_params }
234:         end
235:         format.api  {render_api_ok}
236:       end
237:     else
238:       respond_to do |format|
239:         format.html {render :action => 'edit'}
240:         format.api  {render_validation_errors(@issue)}
241:       end
242:     end
243:   end
244: 
245:   def issue_tab
246:     return render_error :status => 422 unless request.xhr?
247: 
248:     tab = params[:name]
249: 
250:     case tab
251:     when 'time_entries'
252:       @time_entries = @issue.time_entries.visible.preload(:activity, :user).to_a
253:       render :partial => 'issues/tabs/time_entries', :locals => {:time_entries => @time_entries}
254:     when 'changesets'
255:       @changesets = @issue.changesets.visible.preload(:repository, :user).to_a
256:       @changesets.reverse! if User.current.wants_comments_in_reverse_order?
257:       render :partial => 'issues/tabs/changesets', :locals => {:changesets => @changesets, :project => @project}
258:     end
259:   end
260: 
261:   # Bulk edit/copy a set of issues
262:   def bulk_edit
263:     @issues.sort!
264:     @copy = params[:copy].present?
265:     @notes = params[:notes]
266: 
267:     if @copy
268:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:copy_issues, @projects)
269:         raise ::Unauthorized
270:       end
271:     else
272:       unless @issues.all?(&:attributes_editable?)
273:         raise ::Unauthorized
274:       end
275:     end
276: 
277:     edited_issues = Issue.where(:id => @issues.map(&:id)).to_a
278: 
279:     @values_by_custom_field = {}
280:     edited_issues.each do |issue|
281:       issue.custom_field_values.each do |c|
282:         if c.value_present?
283:           @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field] ||= []
284:           @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field] << issue.id
285:         end
286:       end
287:     end
288: 
289:     @allowed_projects = Issue.allowed_target_projects
290:     if params[:issue]
291:       @target_project = @allowed_projects.detect {|p| p.id.to_s == params[:issue][:project_id].to_s}
292:       if @target_project
293:         target_projects = [@target_project]
294:         edited_issues.each {|issue| issue.project = @target_project}
295:       end
296:     end
297:     target_projects ||= @projects
298: 
299:     @trackers = target_projects.map {|p| Issue.allowed_target_trackers(p)}.reduce(:&)
300:     if params[:issue]
301:       @target_tracker = @trackers.detect {|t| t.id.to_s == params[:issue][:tracker_id].to_s}
302:       if @target_tracker
303:         edited_issues.each {|issue| issue.tracker = @target_tracker}
304:       end
305:     end
306: 
307:     if @copy
308:       # Copied issues will get their default statuses
309:       @available_statuses = []
310:     else
311:       @available_statuses = edited_issues.map(&:new_statuses_allowed_to).reduce(:&)
312:     end
313:     if params[:issue]
314:       @target_status = @available_statuses.detect {|t| t.id.to_s == params[:issue][:status_id].to_s}
315:       if @target_status
316:         edited_issues.each {|issue| issue.status = @target_status}
317:       end
318:     end
319: 
320:     edited_issues.each do |issue|
321:       issue.custom_field_values.each do |c|
322:         if c.value_present? && @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field]
323:           @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field].delete(issue.id)
324:         end
325:       end
326:     end
327:     @values_by_custom_field.delete_if {|k, v| v.blank?}
328:     @custom_fields =
329:       edited_issues.map{|i| i.editable_custom_fields}.
330:         reduce(:&).select {|field| field.format.bulk_edit_supported}
331:     @assignables = target_projects.map(&:assignable_users).reduce(:&)
332:     @versions = target_projects.map {|p| p.shared_versions.open}.reduce(:&)
333:     @categories = target_projects.map {|p| p.issue_categories}.reduce(:&)
334:     if @copy
335:       @attachments_present = @issues.detect {|i| i.attachments.any?}.present? &&
336:                                (Setting.copy_attachments_on_issue_copy == 'ask')
337:       @subtasks_present = @issues.detect {|i| !i.leaf?}.present?
338:       @watchers_present = User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @projects) &&
339:                             Watcher.where(:watchable_type => 'Issue',
340:                                           :watchable_id => @issues.map(&:id)).exists?
341:     end
342: 
343:     @safe_attributes = edited_issues.map(&:safe_attribute_names).reduce(:&)
344: 
345:     @issue_params = params[:issue] || {}
346:     @issue_params[:custom_field_values] ||= {}
347:   end
348: 
349:   def bulk_update
350:     @issues.sort!
351:     @copy = params[:copy].present?
352: 
353:     attributes = parse_params_for_bulk_update(params[:issue])
354:     copy_subtasks = (params[:copy_subtasks] == '1')
355:     copy_watchers = (params[:copy_watchers] == '1')
356: 
357:     if @copy
358:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:copy_issues, @projects)
359:         raise ::Unauthorized
360:       end
361: 
362:       target_projects = @projects
363:       if attributes['project_id'].present?
364:         target_projects = Project.where(:id => attributes['project_id']).to_a
365:       end
366:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issues, target_projects)
367:         raise ::Unauthorized
368:       end
369: 
370:       unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @projects)
371:         copy_watchers = false
372:       end
373:     else
374:       unless @issues.all?(&:attributes_editable?)
375:         raise ::Unauthorized
376:       end
377:     end
378: 
379:     unsaved_issues = []
380:     saved_issues = []
381: 
382:     if @copy && copy_subtasks
383:       # Descendant issues will be copied with the parent task
384:       # Don't copy them twice
385:       @issues.reject! {|issue| @issues.detect {|other| issue.is_descendant_of?(other)}}
386:     end
387: 
388:     @issues.each do |orig_issue|
389:       orig_issue.reload
390:       if @copy
391:         issue = orig_issue.copy(
392:           {},
393:           :attachments => copy_attachments?(params[:copy_attachments]),
394:           :subtasks => copy_subtasks,
395:           :watchers => copy_watchers,
396:           :link => link_copy?(params[:link_copy])
397:         )
398:       else
399:         issue = orig_issue
400:       end
401:       journal = issue.init_journal(User.current, params[:notes])
402:       issue.safe_attributes = attributes
403:       call_hook(:controller_issues_bulk_edit_before_save, {:params => params, :issue => issue})
404:       if issue.save
405:         saved_issues << issue
406:       else
407:         unsaved_issues << orig_issue
408:       end
409:     end
410: 
411:     if unsaved_issues.empty?
412:       flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update) unless saved_issues.empty?
413:       if params[:follow]
414:         if @issues.size == 1 && saved_issues.size == 1
415:           redirect_to issue_path(saved_issues.first)
416:         elsif saved_issues.map(&:project).uniq.size == 1
417:           redirect_to project_issues_path(saved_issues.map(&:project).first)
418:         end
419:       else
420:         redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
421:       end
422:     else
423:       @saved_issues = @issues
424:       @unsaved_issues = unsaved_issues
425:       @issues = Issue.visible.where(:id => @unsaved_issues.map(&:id)).to_a
426:       bulk_edit
427:       render :action => 'bulk_edit'
428:     end
429:   end
430: 
431:   def destroy
432:     raise Unauthorized unless @issues.all?(&:deletable?)
433: 
434:     # all issues and their descendants are about to be deleted
435:     issues_and_descendants_ids = Issue.self_and_descendants(@issues).pluck(:id)
436:     time_entries = TimeEntry.where(:issue_id => issues_and_descendants_ids)
437:     @hours = time_entries.sum(:hours).to_f
438: 
439:     if @hours > 0
440:       case params[:todo]
441:       when 'destroy'
442:         # nothing to do
443:       when 'nullify'
444:         if Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')
445:           flash.now[:error] = l(:field_issue) + " " + ::I18n.t('activerecord.errors.messages.blank')
446:           return
447:         else

449:         end
450:       when 'reassign'

452:         if reassign_to.nil?

454:           return

456:           flash.now[:error] = l(:error_cannot_reassign_time_entries_to_an_issue_about_to_be_deleted)
457:           return
458:         else
459:           time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => reassign_to.id, :project_id => reassign_to.project_id)
460:         end
461:       else
462:         # display the destroy form if it's a user request
463:         return unless api_request?
464:       end
465:     end
466:     @issues.each do |issue|
467:       begin
468:         issue.reload.destroy
469:       rescue ::ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound # raised by #reload if issue no longer exists
470:         # nothing to do, issue was already deleted (eg. by a parent)
471:       end
472:     end
473:     respond_to do |format|
474:       format.html do
475:         flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_delete)
476:         redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
477:       end
478:       format.api  {render_api_ok}
479:     end
480:   end

20: class IssueImport < Import
21:   AUTO_MAPPABLE_FIELDS = {
22:     'tracker' => 'field_tracker',
23:     'subject' => 'field_subject',
24:     'description' => 'field_description',
25:     'status' => 'field_status',
26:     'priority' => 'field_priority',
27:     'category' => 'field_category',
28:     'assigned_to' => 'field_assigned_to',
29:     'fixed_version' => 'field_fixed_version',
30:     'is_private' => 'field_is_private',
31:     'parent_issue_id' => 'field_parent_issue',
32:     'start_date' => 'field_start_date',
33:     'due_date' => 'field_due_date',
34:     'estimated_hours' => 'field_estimated_hours',
35:     'done_ratio' => 'field_done_ratio',
36:     'unique_id' => 'field_unique_id',
37:     'relation_duplicates' => 'label_duplicates',
38:     'relation_duplicated' => 'label_duplicated_by',
39:     'relation_blocks' => 'label_blocks',
40:     'relation_blocked' => 'label_blocked_by',
41:     'relation_relates' => 'label_relates_to',
42:     'relation_precedes' => 'label_precedes',
43:     'relation_follows' =>  'label_follows',
44:     'relation_copied_to' => 'label_copied_to',
45:     'relation_copied_from' => 'label_copied_from'
46:   }
47: 
48:   def self.menu_item
49:     :issues
50:   end
51: 
52:   def self.authorized?(user)
53:     user.allowed_to?(:import_issues, nil, :global => true) && user.allowed_to?(:add_issues, nil, :global => true)
54:   end
55: 
56:   # Returns the objects that were imported
57:   def saved_objects
58:     object_ids = saved_items.pluck(:obj_id)
59:     objects = Issue.where(:id => object_ids).order(:id).preload(:tracker, :priority, :status)
60:   end
61: 
62:   # Returns a scope of projects that user is allowed to
63:   # import issue to
64:   def allowed_target_projects
65:     Project.allowed_to(user, :import_issues)
66:   end
67: 
68:   def project
69:     project_id = mapping['project_id'].to_i
70:     allowed_target_projects.find_by_id(project_id) || allowed_target_projects.first
71:   end
72: 
73:   # Returns a scope of trackers that user is allowed to
74:   # import issue to
75:   def allowed_target_trackers
76:     Issue.allowed_target_trackers(project, user)
77:   end
78: 
79:   def tracker
80:     if mapping['tracker'].to_s =~ /\Avalue:(\d+)\z/
81:       tracker_id = $1.to_i
82:       allowed_target_trackers.find_by_id(tracker_id)
83:     end
84:   end
85: 
86:   # Returns true if missing categories should be created during the import
87:   def create_categories?
88:     user.allowed_to?(:manage_categories, project) &&
89:       mapping['create_categories'] == '1'
90:   end
91: 
92:   # Returns true if missing versions should be created during the import
93:   def create_versions?
94:     user.allowed_to?(:manage_versions, project) &&
95:       mapping['create_versions'] == '1'
96:   end
97: 
98:   def mappable_custom_fields
99:     if tracker
100:       issue = Issue.new
101:       issue.project = project
102:       issue.tracker = tracker
103:       issue.editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field)
104:     elsif project
105:       project.all_issue_custom_fields
106:     else
107:       []
108:     end
109:   end
110: 
111:   private
112: 
113:   def build_object(row, item)
114:     issue = Issue.new
115:     issue.author = user
116:     issue.notify = !!ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(settings['notifications'])
117: 
118:     tracker_id = nil
119:     if tracker
120:       tracker_id = tracker.id
121:     elsif tracker_name = row_value(row, 'tracker')
122:       tracker_id = allowed_target_trackers.named(tracker_name).first.try(:id)
123:     end
124: 
125:     attributes = {
126:       'project_id' => mapping['project_id'],
127:       'tracker_id' => tracker_id,
128:       'subject' => row_value(row, 'subject'),
129:       'description' => row_value(row, 'description')
130:     }
131:     if status_name = row_value(row, 'status')
132:       if status_id = IssueStatus.named(status_name).first.try(:id)
133:         attributes['status_id'] = status_id
134:       end
135:     end
136:     issue.send :safe_attributes=, attributes, user
137: 
138:     attributes = {}
139:     if priority_name = row_value(row, 'priority')
140:       if priority_id = IssuePriority.active.named(priority_name).first.try(:id)
141:         attributes['priority_id'] = priority_id
142:       end
143:     end
144:     if issue.project && category_name = row_value(row, 'category')
145:       if category = issue.project.issue_categories.named(category_name).first
146:         attributes['category_id'] = category.id
147:       elsif create_categories?
148:         category = issue.project.issue_categories.build
149:         category.name = category_name
150:         if category.save
151:           attributes['category_id'] = category.id
152:         end
153:       end
154:     end
155:     if assignee_name = row_value(row, 'assigned_to')
156:       if assignee = Principal.detect_by_keyword(issue.assignable_users, assignee_name)
157:         attributes['assigned_to_id'] = assignee.id
158:       end
159:     end
160:     if issue.project && version_name = row_value(row, 'fixed_version')
161:       version =
162:         issue.project.versions.named(version_name).first ||
163:         issue.project.shared_versions.named(version_name).first
164:       if version
165:         attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
166:       elsif create_versions?
167:         version = issue.project.versions.build
168:         version.name = version_name
169:         if version.save
170:           attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
171:         end
172:       end
173:     end
174:     if is_private = row_value(row, 'is_private')
175:       if yes?(is_private)
176:         attributes['is_private'] = '1'
177:       end
178:     end
179:     if parent_issue_id = row_value(row, 'parent_issue_id')
180:       if parent_issue_id.start_with? '#'
181:         # refers to existing issue
182:         attributes['parent_issue_id'] = parent_issue_id[1..-1]
183:       elsif use_unique_id?
184:         # refers to other row with unique id
185:         issue_id = items.where(:unique_id => parent_issue_id).first.try(:obj_id)
186: 
187:         if issue_id
188:           attributes['parent_issue_id'] = issue_id
189:         else
190:           add_callback(parent_issue_id, 'set_as_parent', item.position)
191:         end
192:       elsif /\A\d+\z/.match?(parent_issue_id)
193:         # refers to other row by position
194:         parent_issue_id = parent_issue_id.to_i
195: 
196:         if parent_issue_id > item.position
197:           add_callback(parent_issue_id, 'set_as_parent', item.position)
198:         elsif issue_id = items.where(:position => parent_issue_id).first.try(:obj_id)
199:           attributes['parent_issue_id'] = issue_id
200:         end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # Redmine - project management software

19: 
20: class Changeset < ApplicationRecord
21:   belongs_to :repository
22:   belongs_to :user
23:   has_many :filechanges, :class_name => 'Change', :dependent => :delete_all
24:   has_and_belongs_to_many :issues
25:   has_and_belongs_to_many :parents,
26:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
27:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
28:                           :association_foreign_key => 'parent_id', :foreign_key => 'changeset_id'
29:   has_and_belongs_to_many :children,
30:                           :class_name => "Changeset",
31:                           :join_table => "#{table_name_prefix}changeset_parents#{table_name_suffix}",
32:                           :association_foreign_key => 'changeset_id', :foreign_key => 'parent_id'
33: 
34:   acts_as_event(
35:     :title => proc {|o| o.title},
36:     :description => :long_comments,
37:     :datetime => :committed_on,
38:     :url =>
39:       proc do |o|
40:         {:controller => 'repositories', :action => 'revision',
41:          :id => o.repository.project,
42:          :repository_id => o.repository.identifier_param, :rev => o.identifier}
43:       end
44:   )
45:   acts_as_searchable :columns => 'comments',
46:                      :preload => {:repository => :project},
47:                      :project_key => "#{Repository.table_name}.project_id",
48:                      :date_column => :committed_on
49: 
50:   acts_as_activity_provider :timestamp => "#{table_name}.committed_on",
51:                             :author_key => :user_id,
52:                             :scope => proc {preload(:user, {:repository => :project})}
53: 
54:   validates_presence_of :repository_id, :revision, :committed_on, :commit_date
55:   validates_uniqueness_of :revision, :scope => :repository_id, :case_sensitive => true
56:   validates_uniqueness_of :scmid, :scope => :repository_id, :allow_nil => true, :case_sensitive => true
57: 
58:   scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
59:     joins(:repository => :project).
60:     where(Project.allowed_to_condition(args.shift || User.current, :view_changesets, *args))
61:   end)
62: 
63:   before_create :before_create_cs
64:   after_create :scan_for_issues
65: 
66:   def revision=(r)
67:     write_attribute :revision, (r.nil? ? nil : r.to_s)
68:   end
69: 
70:   # Returns the identifier of this changeset; depending on repository backends
71:   def identifier
72:     if repository.class.respond_to? :changeset_identifier
73:       repository.class.changeset_identifier self
74:     else
75:       revision.to_s
76:     end
77:   end
78: 
79:   def committed_on=(date)
80:     self.commit_date = date
81:     super
82:   end
83: 
84:   # Returns the readable identifier
85:   def format_identifier
86:     if repository.class.respond_to? :format_changeset_identifier
87:       repository.class.format_changeset_identifier self
88:     else
89:       identifier
90:     end
91:   end
92: 
93:   def project
94:     repository.project
95:   end
96: 
97:   def author
98:     user || committer.to_s.split('<').first
99:   end
100: 
101:   def before_create_cs
102:     self.committer = self.class.to_utf8(self.committer, repository.repo_log_encoding)
103:     self.comments =
104:       self.class.normalize_comments(self.comments, repository.repo_log_encoding)
105:     self.user = repository.find_committer_user(self.committer)
106:   end
107: 
108:   def scan_for_issues
109:     scan_comment_for_issue_ids
110:   end
111: 
112:   TIMELOG_RE = /
113:     (
114:     ((\d+)(h|hours?))((\d+)(m|min)?)?
115:     |
116:     ((\d+)(h|hours?|m|min))
117:     |
118:     (\d+):(\d+)
119:     |
120:     (\d+([.,]\d+)?)h?
121:     )
122:     /x
123: 
124:   def scan_comment_for_issue_ids
125:     return if comments.blank?
126: 
127:     # keywords used to reference issues
128:     ref_keywords = Setting.commit_ref_keywords.downcase.split(",").collect(&:strip)
129:     ref_keywords_any = ref_keywords.delete('*')
130: 
131:     # keywords used to fix issues
132:     fix_keywords = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.pluck('keywords').flatten.compact
133:     kw_regexp = (ref_keywords + fix_keywords).collect{|kw| Regexp.escape(kw)}.join("|")
134: 
135:     referenced_issues = []
136:     regexp =
137:       %r{
138:         ([\s(\[,-]|^)((#{kw_regexp})[\s:]+)?
139:         (\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?([\s,;&]+\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?)*)
140:         (?=[[:punct:]]|\s|<|$)
141:       }xi
142:     comments.scan(regexp) do |match|
143:       action = match[2].to_s.downcase
144:       refs   = match[3]
145:       next unless action.present? || ref_keywords_any
146: 
147:       refs.scan(/#(\d+)(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?/o).each do |m|
148:         issue = find_referenced_issue_by_id(m[0].to_i)
149:         hours = m[2]
150:         if issue && !issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
151:           referenced_issues << issue
152:           # Don't update issues or log time when importing old commits
153:           unless repository.created_on && committed_on && committed_on < repository.created_on
154:             fix_issue(issue, action) if fix_keywords.include?(action)
155:             log_time(issue, hours) if hours && Setting.commit_logtime_enabled?
156:           end
157:         end
158:       end
159:     end
160: 
161:     referenced_issues.uniq!
162:     self.issues = referenced_issues unless referenced_issues.empty?
163:   end
164: 
165:   def short_comments
166:     @short_comments || split_comments.first
167:   end
168: 
169:   def long_comments
170:     @long_comments || split_comments.last
171:   end
172: 
173:   def text_tag(ref_project=nil)
174:     repo = ""
175:     if repository && repository.identifier.present?
176:       repo = "#{repository.identifier}|"
177:     end
178:     tag = scmid? ? "commit:#{repo}#{scmid}" : "#{repo}r#{revision}"
179:     if ref_project && project && ref_project != project
180:       tag = "#{project.identifier}:#{tag}"
181:     end
182:     tag
183:   end
184: 
185:   # Returns the title used for the changeset in the activity/search results
186:   def title
187:     repo = (repository && repository.identifier.present?) ? " (#{repository.identifier})" : ''
188:     comm = short_comments.blank? ? '' : (': ' + short_comments)
189:     "#{l(:label_revision)} #{format_identifier}#{repo}#{comm}"
190:   end
191: 
192:   # Returns the previous changeset
193:   def previous
194:     @previous ||= repository&.previous_changeset(self)
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Returns the next changeset
198:   def next
199:     @next ||= repository&.next_changeset(self)
200:   end
201: 
202:   # Creates a new Change from it's common parameters
203:   def create_change(change)
204:     Change.create(:changeset     => self,
205:                   :action        => change[:action],
206:                   :path          => change[:path],
207:                   :from_path     => change[:from_path],
208:                   :from_revision => change[:from_revision])
209:   end
210: 
211:   # Finds an issue that can be referenced by the commit message
212:   def find_referenced_issue_by_id(id)
213:     return nil if id.blank?
214: 
215:     issue = Issue.find_by_id(id.to_i)
216:     if Setting.commit_cross_project_ref?
217:       # all issues can be referenced/fixed
218:     elsif issue
219:       # issue that belong to the repository project, a subproject or a parent project only
220:       unless issue.project &&
221:                 (project == issue.project || project.is_ancestor_of?(issue.project) ||
222:                  project.is_descendant_of?(issue.project))
223:         issue = nil
224:       end
225:     end
226:     issue
227:   end
228: 
229:   private
230: 
231:   # Returns true if the issue is already linked to the same commit
232:   # from a different repository
233:   def issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
234:     repository.same_commits_in_scope(issue.changesets, self).any?
235:   end
236: 
237:   # Updates the +issue+ according to +action+
238:   def fix_issue(issue, action)
239:     # the issue may have been updated by the closure of another one (eg. duplicate)
240:     issue.reload
241:     # don't change the status is the issue is closed
242:     return if issue.closed?
243: 
244:     journal = issue.init_journal(user || User.anonymous,
245:                                  ll(Setting.default_language,
246:                                     :text_status_changed_by_changeset,
247:                                     text_tag(issue.project)))
248:     rule = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.detect do |rule|
249:       rule['keywords'].include?(action) &&
250:         (rule['if_tracker_id'].blank? || rule['if_tracker_id'] == issue.tracker_id.to_s)
251:     end
252:     if rule
253:       issue.assign_attributes rule.slice(*Issue.attribute_names)
254:     end
255:     Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_changeset_scan_commit_for_issue_ids_pre_issue_update,
256:                             {:changeset => self, :issue => issue, :action => action})
257: 
258:     if issue.changes.any?
259:       unless issue.save
260:         logger.warn("Issue ##{issue.id} could not be saved by changeset #{id}: #{issue.errors.full_messages}") if logger
261:       end
262:     else
263:       issue.clear_journal
264:     end
265:     issue
266:   end
267: 
268:   def log_time(issue, hours)
269:     time_entry =
270:       TimeEntry.new(
271:         :user => user,
272:         :hours => hours,
273:         :issue => issue,
274:         :spent_on => commit_date,
275:         :comments => l(:text_time_logged_by_changeset, :value => text_tag(issue.project),
276:                        :locale => Setting.default_language)
277:       )
278:     if activity = issue.project.commit_logtime_activity
279:       time_entry.activity = activity
280:     end
281: 
282:     unless time_entry.save
283:       logger.warn("TimeEntry could not be created by changeset #{id}: #{time_entry.errors.full_messages}") if logger
284:     end
285:     time_entry
286:   end

288:   def split_comments
289:     comments =~ /\A(.+?)\r?\n(.*)$/m
290:     @short_comments = $1 || comments
291:     @long_comments = $2.to_s.strip
292:     [@short_comments, @long_comments]
293:   end

297:     # Strips and reencodes a commit log before insertion into the database
